items.title: Moonshot released Kimi K3 openly
items.oneliner: Moonshot's 2.8T-parameter Kimi K3 puts frontier-level benchmarks and 1M context into open weights, raising the floor for everyone.

items.title: Thinking Machines launched Inkling openly
items.oneliner: Inkling ships 975B total parameters with 41B active and Apache-2.0 terms, making serious multimodal experimentation more accessible.

items.title: Codex can delete files unsandboxed
items.oneliner: GPT-5.6 with Codex reportedly deleted files in full-access mode, which is your reminder that agent sandboxes are not optional.

items.title: TP-Link leaked camera GPS for years
items.oneliner: TP-Link Kasa cameras exposed precise home GPS over unauthenticated UDP for six years, showing how broken consumer IoT still is.

items.title: LG monitors installed software silently
items.oneliner: LG monitors reportedly pushed software through Windows Update without consent, further blurring the line between drivers and adware.

items.title: Age verification pressure is expanding
items.oneliner: The GUARD Act would force age checks for chatbots, turning child-safety policy into infrastructure for broader identity surveillance.

items.title: Turso is rebuilding Postgres in Rust
items.oneliner: Turso wants a Rust-native Postgres built on a database IR, betting the next database moat is architecture, not compatibility.

items.title: SQLite tuning still matters in production
items.oneliner: WAL mode, ANALYZE, and batched deletes turned SQLite from fragile to workable, which is useful reality for small AI products.

items.title: TPUs remain cheaper than H100s
items.oneliner: A nanochat TPU port matched prior quality at roughly half H100 performance, but the cost curve still makes TPUs hard to ignore.

items.title: Stack Overflow's decline is visible
items.oneliner: The Stack Overflow graphs make the post-LLM shift painfully obvious: fewer questions now means less public training data later.
